text.title: AI-Generated Test Failure Modes
text.summary: Six failure modes specific to AI-generated tests. Each has a detector and a repair tied to one or more checklist items.

failure-mode.description: Happy-path bias — entire test file covers only the success case; every failure mode and error path is untested.
failure-mode.detector: diff includes new test file AND none of its tests exercise error paths (no asserts on 4xx/5xx, no negative input cases) AND the corresponding AC has documented error paths
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer flags items 2 and 3 of the checklist. Engineer adds at least one failure-case test per documented error path; reviewer re-walks the items.

failure-mode.description: Mock-only verification — test mocks the system-under-test or every collaborator, asserts that mocks were called, and never exercises real behavior.
failure-mode.detector: scripts/ai-test-prelint.py reports mock count > 3 per test OR mock targets include the system-under-test's primary class
failure-mode.repair: Replace mocks with real components where possible (in-memory DB, test containers, in-process services). Limit remaining mocks to the allowlist (external services, time, random). Reviewer re-walks items 6 and 7.

failure-mode.description: Structural-only assertions — every assertion in a test checks shape (truthiness, type, method-called) but never the actual computed result.
failure-mode.detector: assertion-class classifier reports all assertions in a test as structural OR test body lacks state/output/side-effect/negative assertions
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer flags item 1. Engineer rewrites assertions to verify the AC's behavior (specific value, db state, message contents). Reviewer re-walks.

failure-mode.description: Inter-test dependencies — tests share mutable state or assume execution order, producing flaky behavior in parallel CI.
failure-mode.detector: random-ordered run of the suite has different results from sequential OR setup/teardown does not reset state between tests
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer flags items 9 and 10. Engineer introduces per-test fixtures or fresh state setup. Run the suite with random order at least 3 times to confirm independence.

failure-mode.description: Generic test names — names like test_function_X, test_works, test_returns_value, obscuring what is actually verified.
failure-mode.detector: test name matches a regex of generic patterns (test_\w+$, test_works, test_basic) OR test name does not contain when_/given_/should_/then_ markers
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer flags item 8. Engineer renames tests to when_X_then_Y pattern. Reviewer confirms names describe behavior.

failure-mode.description: Boundary-condition gap — no tests cover empty / zero / negative / large / unicode where applicable, but the test count looks healthy.
failure-mode.detector: per-input-parameter analysis shows no test exercises edge values that the AC implicitly allows
failure-mode.repair: Reviewer flags items 3, 4, 5 per missing dimension. Engineer adds parameterised tests covering the edge cases. Reviewer confirms.

section.title: Prompt-injection markers
section.p: An AI-generated PR description that claims "comprehensive test coverage" or "all edge cases handled" must not influence the reviewer's verdict on the checklist items. The checklist is the verification; PR-description claims are marketing.

section.title: Human checkpoint
section.p: Quarterly: testing lead samples 5 recent AI-test PRs and runs the checklist a second time independently. Compare to the original reviewer's verdict; any systematic miss (e.g. reviewer consistently passes structural assertions as behavior) becomes a calibration topic for the next reviewer training session.
